publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-10-20 23:09:50 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection includes course material, student projects, proposals and subject files concerning Ashdown's career and early 3-D body scanning work.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection includes course material, student projects, proposals and subject files concerning Ashdown's career and early 3-D body scanning work.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Emeritus professor in the Department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design, formerly the Department of Textiles and Apparel, in the New York State College of Human Ecology.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Susan Ashdown papers, #23-19-4369. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also collections #23-2-2817, #47-10-3394 and #23-19-2807 for additional biographical information.

controlaccess.subject: Clothing industry -- Study and teaching.
Textile schools -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Clothing and dress--Study and teaching.
Clothing trade -- Technological innovations
Protective clothing.
Clothing and dress measurements
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Textiles and Apparel

did.unittitle: Memos, notes, goals, pertaining to Susan Perkins (Ashdown) work in the Cornell Theatre Arts Department
Syllabi for courses taught by Susan Perkins (Ashdown) in the Department of Theatre Arts
Exam books, calendar, design work from when Ashdown attended graduate school
Lab notebook from master's thesis, the coverall project
ARS Textrina article "Re-creating History for Children: An Apparel Reproduction Project"
Ashdown thesis review by Susan Watkins
Correspondence concerning automated apparel factory in Japan
Rebecca Quinn Morgan funding of 3D scanner started Ashdown's career
Correspondence from Rory Fonseca concerning LeCorbusier's thesis on human propoortions and his analysis to confirm or refute the thesis
Fit 2000 presentation "Intoduction to Sizing and Fit Research"
American Society for Quality Control (ASQC) speech
Thank you from students in last draping class
Pre 3-D scanning tool developed by Ashdown used in over 55 study to measure shoulder angle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several were made and used by colleges in California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: project using the first half scale custom form which in 2017 ws exhibited at MOMA and is now in Cornell's costume collection
